ui_hierarchy.description: Candidate parsed from current Android UI hierarchy XML or Appium page source.

provided.description: Locator already present in trusted input or resolved state.

tie_breakers: prefer selectors based on stable native Android attributes
prefer previously verified locators when current page evidence is similar

source_trust.provided: accepted when it comes from trusted resolved state
source_trust.element_memory: high confidence if previously successful on a similar page
source_trust.ui_hierarchy: current screen evidence, must be checked for uniqueness

current_page_uniqueness.unique_match: strong bonus when the locator matches exactly one current UI node
current_page_uniqueness.duplicate_match: penalty when id/text/content-desc appears on multiple current UI nodes
current_page_uniqueness.unknown: do not assume uniqueness without current page evidence

selector_specificity.combined_android_uiautomator: strong when UiSelector combines stable attributes such as resourceId + text/description
selector_specificity.single_attribute: acceptable only if the attribute is unique or from a trusted source
selector_specificity.xpath: fallback unless it targets stable attributes instead of hierarchy position

supported_locator_types.id: Appium/Selenium resource id locator. Prefer when unique on the current page or trusted by manual mapping/memory.
supported_locator_types.android_uiautomator: Android UiSelector expression. Prefer when it combines stable native Android attributes, especially when a plain id is duplicated.
supported_locator_types.accessibility_id: Appium accessibility id locator, usually content-desc.
supported_locator_types.text: Project shorthand converted to XPath text equality at execution time.
supported_locator_types.xpath: XPath locator, should be used as a fallback because hierarchy paths can be brittle.

assert_checked: checkbox, radio button, switch, or toggle element
assert_enabled: interactive element with stable id or label
assert_selected: tab, radio button, list item, or selectable element
assert_text_equals: text-bearing element with stable id, text, or content-desc
assert_text_contains: text-bearing element with stable id, text, or content-desc
wait_gone: stable loading marker, dialog, or transient message
assert_not_visible: stable absent-state marker, dialog, or error message
swipe: scrollable container when a target is supplied
scroll: scrollable container when a target is supplied
drag_and_drop: draggable source and stable destination target
pinch: zoomable map, image, or canvas area
zoom: zoomable map, image, or canvas area
